name: CLKSPLIT_f
description: Clock Split block - forks an event signal into two identical event signals
xmlExample:
<SplitBlock id="b1" parent="0:2:0" interfaceFunctionName="CLKSPLIT_f" blockType="d" dependsOnU="0" dependsOnT="0" simulationFunctionName="split" simulationFunctionType="DEFAULT" style="CLKSPLIT_f">
    <ScilabDouble as="exprs" height="0" width="0" />
    <ScilabDouble as="realParameters" height="0" width="0" />
    <ScilabDouble as="integerParameters" height="0" width="0" />
    <Array as="objectsParameters" scilabClass="ScilabList" />
    <ScilabInteger as="nbZerosCrossing" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32">
        <data line="0" column="0" value="0" />
    </ScilabInteger>
    <ScilabInteger as="nmode" height="1" width="1" intPrecision="sci_int32">
        <data line="0" column="0" value="0" />
    </ScilabInteger>
    <ScilabDouble as="state" height="0" width="0" />
    <ScilabDouble as="dState" height="0" width="0" />
    <Array as="oDState" scilabClass="ScilabList" />
    <Array as="equations" scilabClass="ScilabList" />
    <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="380.71" y="172.0" width="0.333" height="0.333" />
</SplitBlock>
anomalies: Near-zero width/height (0.33) - represents a junction point
Splits event/clock signals (no explicit data ports)
criticalRules: Exactly one control port and two command ports
commonUses: Forking clock signals to multiple blocks
Creating feedback loops in event-driven systems
